title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: micromort unit risk representing one---million chance death. used measure acute risks—risks can kill immediately (e.g., skydiving, driving).
code: # 1 in 10,000 chance of death = 100 micromorts prob <- 1/10000 as_micromort(prob) #> [1] 100  # Compare common risks (sortable table - click column headers) risks <- common_risks() DT::datatable(   risks,   caption = "Common risks in micromorts (click column headers to sort, use search box to filter)",   filter = "top",   options = list(pageLength = 10, scrollX = TRUE),   rownames = FALSE )

title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: Using plot_risks(), can see relative magnitude different activities logarithmic scale.

title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: micromorts measure sudden death, microlives measure impact chronic habits life expectancy. microlife represents 30-minute change life expectancy. Common chronic risks: * Smoking 1 cigarette: -1 microlife (approx 1 micromort equivalent risk) * 5kg overweight: -1 microlife per day * First 20 mins moderate exercise: +2 microlives
code: # If smoking 20 cigarettes a day costs 1 microlife each (approx 30 mins) daily_loss_minutes <- 20 * 30 daily_loss_microlives <- as_microlife(daily_loss_minutes) print(daily_loss_microlives) # 20 microlives lost per day #> [1] 20

title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: Micromorts (acute risk) microlives (chronic attrition) measure different phenomena, can approximately converted: Key relationship: 1 micromort ≈ 0.7 microlives (assuming 40 years remaining life expectancy) Mathematical derivation: microlives column common_risks() uses conversion microlives = micromorts × 0.7.
code:
# 1 micromort = 1-in-a-million death probability # Expected life lost = probability × remaining life expectancy remaining_years <- 40 prob_death <- 1e-6  # 1 micromort  # Life lost in years life_lost_years <- prob_death * remaining_years cat("Life lost (years):", life_lost_years, "
") #> Life lost (years): 4e-05  # Convert to minutes life_lost_minutes <- life_lost_years * 365.25 * 24 * 60 cat("Life lost (minutes):", round(life_lost_minutes, 1), "
") #> Life lost (minutes): 21  # 1 microlife = 30 minutes microlives_equivalent <- life_lost_minutes / 30 cat("Microlives equivalent:", round(microlives_equivalent, 2), "
") #> Microlives equivalent: 0.7

title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: Use micromorts comparing discrete events immediate risk (surgery, travel, sports) Use microlives assessing lifestyle changes time (quitting smoking, weight loss) Convert making policy decisions involve acute chronic risks

title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: Value Statistical Life (VSL) monetary value used justify safety spending. value individual life, aggregate willingness pay small risk reductions. Example: safety feature costs $50 saves 1 life 100,000 people (10 micromorts), worth ? Cost per micromort saved = $50 / 10 = $5. VSL = $10M, 1 micromort = $10. Since $5 < $10, cost-effective.
code: # Standard VSL of $10M implies $10 per micromort value_of_micromort(vsl = 10000000) #> [1] 10  # Higher VSL implies higher safety spending value_of_micromort(vsl = 15000000) #> [1] 15

title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: LLE estimates average time lost lifespan due specific risk. 1---million risk (1 micromort), LLE tiny.
code: # Loss of life expectancy from 1 micromort (assuming 40 years remaining) lle_minutes <- lle(prob = 1/1e6, life_expectancy = 40) print(lle_minutes) #> [1] 21.0384 #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "micromort_lle" "numeric"       #> attr(,"units") #> [1] "minutes" # Result is in minutes. 1 micromort ~ 21 minutes lost? # No, 1 micromort = 1e-6 * 40 years = 40e-6 years = ~21 minutes # Wait, check calculation: # 40 years * 365.25 days * 24 hours * 60 minutes = ~21 million minutes # 1e-6 * 21 million = ~21 minutes

what: 6. Complementary Metrics: QALY, DALY, and Morbidity
title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: Micromorts microlives focus mortality. many conditions (like common cold) cause significant quality life loss without fatal. Complementary metrics capture morbidity burden.

previous_headings: 6. Complementary Metrics: QALY, DALY, and Morbidity
title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: Measures years life adjusted quality. 1 QALY = 1 year perfect health. Health states weighted 0 (death) 1 (perfect health) year chronic pain 0.7 quality = 0.7 QALYs Used assess cost-effectiveness medical interventions (e.g., £20,000-30,000 per QALY threshold UK)

previous_headings: 6. Complementary Metrics: QALY, DALY, and Morbidity
title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: Measures disease burden sum : YLL (Years Life Lost): premature mortality YLD (Years Lived Disability): morbidity, weighted disability severity Formula: DALY = YLL + YLD fatal diseases like COVID-19, YLL dominates. non-fatal conditions like common cold, YLD dominates.

previous_headings: 6. Complementary Metrics: QALY, DALY, and Morbidity
title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: common cold doesn’t kill many people, millions work/school days lost annually. capture microlife-like metric? Proposed “Microburden”: Population-level quality life lost per million person-days.
code: # Example: Common cold burden calculation # Disability weight for common cold: ~0.006 (WHO GBD) # Average duration: 5-7 days # Episodes per person per year: ~2-3  cold_disability_weight <- 0.006 cold_duration_days <- 6 episodes_per_year <- 2.5  # QALD (Quality-Adjusted Life Days) lost per episode qald_per_episode <- cold_disability_weight * cold_duration_days  # Annual burden per person (in days of perfect health equivalent) annual_burden_days <- qald_per_episode * episodes_per_year cat("Annual quality burden (QALD/person):", round(annual_burden_days, 4), "\n") #> Annual quality burden (QALD/person): 0.09  # For 1 million people population <- 1e6 total_qald_lost <- annual_burden_days * population cat("Population burden (QALD/million):", format(total_qald_lost, big.mark = ","), "\n") #> Population burden (QALD/million): 90,000

previous_headings: 6. Complementary Metrics: QALY, DALY, and Morbidity
title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: Global Burden Disease: ghdx.healthdata.org Spiegelhalter D (2012). BMJ 2012;345:e8223. doi:10.1136/bmj.e8223

title: Introduction to Micromorts and Risk Visualization
text: micromort package helps translate abstract probabilities concrete units better decision-making. comparing acute risks (micromorts), chronic risks (microlives), quality--life metrics (QALYs, DALYs), individuals policymakers can make informed choices risk trade-offs.

what: 1. The Core Philosophy: Compare Apples to Oranges
title: Palatable Units: The Spiegelhalter Philosophy
text: goal palatable units create common currency risk. allows us strip away emotional “dread factor” scary-sounding events compare rationally mundane activities.

previous_headings: 1. The Core Philosophy: Compare Apples to Oranges
title: Palatable Units: The Spiegelhalter Philosophy
text: Micromort: 1---million chance acute death (sudden event). Microlife: 30 minutes life expectancy lost/gained (chronic attrition).

title: Palatable Units: The Spiegelhalter Philosophy
text: micromort measures acute hazard: immediate probability event causing death. Normalization: Risk normalized per event (per unit distance), independent event’s duration. Skydiving: risk ~7 micromorts per jump. Whether freefall lasts 30 seconds 60 seconds secondary event jumping. Scuba Diving: risk ~5 micromorts per dive. 30-minute dive 45-minute dive treated single “dive events” broad statistics, though technically longer exposure increases risk. Anesthesia: ~10 micromorts per operation.

title: Palatable Units: The Spiegelhalter Philosophy
text: following table demonstrates disparate activities can compared using micromort. Comparison: Riding motorcycle just 60 miles carries acute death risk (~10 micromorts) undergoing general anesthesia.
code: risks <- tibble::tribble(   ~Activity, ~Micromorts, ~Category, ~Context,   "Motorcycling 60 miles", 10, "Travel", "High-risk transport",   "General Anesthesia", 10, "Medical", "Per emergency operation",   "Skydiving", 7, "Sport", "Per jump (USPA stats)",   "Running a Marathon", 7, "Sport", "Cardiovascular stress per race",   "Scuba Diving", 5, "Sport", "Per dive (PADI certified)",   "Living (age 30)", 1, "Baseline", "Daily background risk for a young adult",   "Driving 230 miles", 1, "Travel", "Routine long-distance drive",   "Walking 20 miles", 1, "Travel", "Pedestrian accident risk" )  DT::datatable(   risks,   caption = "Risk comparison in Micromorts (click column headers to sort)",   filter = "top",   options = list(pageLength = 10, dom = "Bfrtip"),   rownames = FALSE )

title: Palatable Units: The Spiegelhalter Philosophy
text: micromorts measure sudden death (Hazard), Microlives measure chronic attrition: rate “using ” life expectancy. Definition: 1 Microlife = 30 minutes life expectancy. Normalization: Risk normalized per day maintaining habit. Unit Attrition: “unit” expected lifespan. -1 Microlife means expected lifespan shrunk 30 minutes. Time Horizon: Continuous. smoke 20 cigarettes day, losing 10 microlives (5 hours) every single day.

title: Palatable Units: The Spiegelhalter Philosophy
text: Clarification: value -1 Microlife loss (attrition). effectively means aging 30 minutes faster normal. year, -1 daily deficit sums ~180 hours (7.5 days) lost life.
code: chronic_risks_table <- tibble::tribble(   ~Habit, ~Impact_Microlives, ~Time_Effect,   "Smoking 2 cigarettes", -1, "Lose 30 minutes life expectancy",   "Being 5kg overweight", -1, "Lose 30 minutes (per day of being overweight)",   "Second pint of beer", -1, "Lose 30 minutes",   "First 20 mins exercise", +1, "Gain 30 minutes life expectancy",   "Eating portion of fruit", +1, "Gain 30 minutes" )  DT::datatable(   chronic_risks_table,   caption = "Impact of daily habits in Microlives (click column headers to sort)",   filter = "top",   options = list(pageLength = 10, dom = "Bfrtip"),   rownames = FALSE )

title: Palatable Units: The Spiegelhalter Philosophy
text: Spiegelhalter advocates Logarithmic Risk Ladder. visualization helps placing rare risks (like asteroid impacts terrorism) context daily risks. Log Scale? risks span vast orders magnitude (1 10 1 10 million). Interpretation: “100% increase” rare risk (e.g., eating bacon increasing bowel cancer risk) might look huge headlines often negligible ladder compared baseline risk driving.
code: # Using the package's built-in plotting function which implements this ladder concept plot_risks()

title: Palatable Units: The Spiegelhalter Philosophy
text: David Spiegelhalter focuses concepts rather specific software, following R packages align mission clear risk communication: riskCommunicator: Designed public health provide interpretable effect measures (risk differences, number needed treat) rather abstract regression coefficients. ggplot2: standard creating custom visuals like Risk Ladders icon arrays. micromort (package): Specifically built implement palatable units framework.

title: Palatable Units: The Spiegelhalter Philosophy
text: Spiegelhalter, D., & Blastland, M. (2013). Norm Chronicles: Stories numbers danger. Profile Books. Spiegelhalter, D. (2019). Art Statistics: Learning Data. Pelican.

title: Micromort - Curated Risk Datasets
text: goal micromort provide tools quantify, compare, visualize risks using standardized units micromorts (acute risk death) microlives (chronic impact life expectancy).

title: Micromort - Curated Risk Datasets
text: can install development version micromort GitHub :

title: Micromort - Curated Risk Datasets
text: project provides reproducible Nix environment.
code: # Enter project shell ./default.sh  # Start R R

title: Micromort - Curated Risk Datasets
text: Micromort: one---million chance death. Used acute risks (e.g., skydiving). Microlife: 30-minute change life expectancy. Used chronic risks (e.g., smoking). VSL (Value Statistical Life): Economic value used justify safety spending.

title: Micromort - Curated Risk Datasets
text: risky skydiving compared driving?
code: # library(micromort)  # Convert probability to micromorts as_micromort(1/1000000) # 1 micromort #> [1] 1  # Common risks head(common_risks()) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   activity                risk_micromorts category #>   <chr>                             <dbl> <chr>    #> 1 Skydiving (one jump)                7   Sport    #> 2 Running a marathon                  7   Sport    #> 3 Scuba diving (per dive)             5   Sport    #> 4 Skiing (one day)                    0.5 Sport    #> 5 Motorcycling (60 miles)            10   Travel   #> 6 Walking (20 miles)                  1   Travel

title: Micromort - Curated Risk Datasets
text: much life expectancy lose smoking?
code: # 1 cigarette = approx 1 microlife lost (30 mins) # Pack a day (20 cigs) = 20 microlives = 10 hours lost per day daily_loss <- as_microlife(20 * 30) print(daily_loss) #> [1] 20

title: Micromort - Curated Risk Datasets
text: (Tree view requires fs package)

title: Acute Risks Dataset — acute_risks
text: curated dataset acute mortality risks measured micromorts. One micromort equals one---million chance death.

title: Acute Risks Dataset — acute_risks
text: tibble 62 rows 15 columns: record_id Unique record identifier (source_id + sequence) activity Human-readable activity name activity_normalized Standardized activity name grouping micromorts Risk micromorts (1 = one---million death risk) microlives Equivalent microlives (micromorts × 0.7) category Activity category (Sport, Travel, Medical, etc.) period Time period risk (per event, per day, per year) period_normalized Standardized period (event, day, week, month, year) age_group Applicable age group (, 18-49, 65+, etc.) geography Geographic scope (global, US, UK, etc.) year Year data collection source_id Source identifier (foreign key risk_sources) source_url Direct URL source confidence Data quality level (high, medium, low) last_accessed Date data retrieved

title: Annual Risk Budget — annual_risk_budget
text: Calculate total annual micromort exposure list activities.

title: Annual Risk Budget — annual_risk_budget
text: activities Named numeric vector activity frequencies per year. Names match activity names acute risks dataset. age Optional age baseline risk calculation

title: Annual Risk Budget — annual_risk_budget
text: tibble risk budget breakdown including: activity Activity name frequency Times per year micromorts_per Micromorts per occurrence annual_micromorts Total annual micromorts pct_of_total Percentage total risk budget

title: Chronic Risks Dataset — chronic_risks
text: curated dataset chronic lifestyle factors measured microlives. One microlife equals 30 minutes life expectancy gained lost. dataset chronic lifestyle factors impact life expectancy, measured microlives (30 minutes life expectancy per day).

title: Chronic Risks Dataset — chronic_risks
text: tibble 22 rows 12 columns: record_id Unique record identifier factor Human-readable factor name factor_normalized Standardized factor name grouping microlives_per_day Daily impact microlives (+/- 30 min units) direction Effect direction: "gain" "loss" category Factor category (Diet, Exercise, Smoking, etc.) description Detailed description factor annual_effect_days Days life gained/lost per year source_id Source identifier source_url Direct URL source confidence Data quality level last_accessed Date data retrieved

title: Chronic Risks Dataset — chronic_risks
text: Spiegelhalter D (2012). "Using speed ageing 'microlives' communicate effects lifetime habits environment." BMJ 2012;345:e8223. doi:10.1136/bmj.e8223

title: Chronic Risks Dataset — chronic_risks
text: tibble columns: factor, microlives_per_day, category, direction, annual_effect_days, source_url.

title: Chronic Risks Dataset — chronic_risks
text: Positive values indicate life expectancy gains; negative values indicate losses. Based framework introduced David Spiegelhalter (2012). Positive values indicate life expectancy gains; negative values indicate losses. Effects cumulative lifetime adult exposure (~57 years).

title: Chronic Risks Dataset — chronic_risks
text: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microlife Spiegelhalter D (2012). "Using speed ageing 'microlives' communicate effects lifetime habits environment." BMJ 2012;345:e8223. doi:10.1136/bmj.e8223 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microlife https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23247978/

title: Acute Risks in Micromorts — common_risks
text: comprehensive dataset activities associated acute mortality risk micromorts, calculated microlives source references.

title: Acute Risks in Micromorts — common_risks
text: tibble columns: activity Activity name micromorts Risk micromorts (1 = 1---million death probability) microlives Equivalent microlives (micromorts × 0.7) category Activity category period Human-readable period description period_type Normalized period type: "event", "day", "hour", "year", "period" period_days Typical duration days (cross-activity comparison) source_url Data source URL

title: Acute Risks in Micromorts — common_risks
text: Micromort: one---million chance death (acute risk). Microlife: 30 minutes life expectancy lost. Data sources: Wikipedia, micromorts.rip, CDC MMWR, academic literature.

title: Acute Risks in Micromorts — common_risks
text: Howard RA (1980). "Making Life Death Decisions." Schwing & Albers (eds), Societal Risk Assessment: Safe Safe Enough? https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Micromort https://micromorts.rip/

title: Compare Lifestyle Interventions — compare_interventions
text: Compare impact multiple lifestyle changes microlives. Uses chronic risks dataset calculate daily, annual, lifetime effects interventions.

title: Compare Lifestyle Interventions — compare_interventions
text: interventions Named list interventions. element : factor Name chronic risk factor (must match chronic_risks) change Numeric change multiplier (e.g., -1 remove factor)

title: Compare Lifestyle Interventions — compare_interventions
text: tibble comparing effects intervention: intervention Name intervention factor Original factor name original_ml_per_day Original microlives per day change Change multiplier applied net_ml_per_day Net microlives gained/lost per day annual_days Days life gained/lost per year lifetime_years Years life gained/lost 57 years

title: Compare Lifestyle Interventions — compare_interventions
code: # Compare quitting smoking vs losing weight compare_interventions(list(   "Quit 10 cigarettes/day" = list(factor = "Smoking 10 cigarettes", change = -1),   "Lose 5kg" = list(factor = "Being 5 kg overweight", change = -1) )) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   intervention      factor original_ml_per_day change net_ml_per_day annual_days #>   <chr>             <chr>                <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 Quit 10 cigarett… Smoki…                  -5     -1             -5       -38   #> 2 Lose 5kg          Being…                  -1     -1             -1        -7.6 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: lifetime_years <dbl>

title: COVID-19 Vaccine Relative Risks — covid_vaccine_rr
text: Mortality risk comparison vaccinated unvaccinated populations Omicron BA.4/BA.5 period (Sep-Dec 2022).

title: COVID-19 Vaccine Relative Risks — covid_vaccine_rr
text: tibble vaccination status, death rates, micromorts, microlives, relative risk compared bivalent booster recipients.

title: COVID-19 Vaccine Relative Risks — covid_vaccine_rr
text: Data source: CDC MMWR Vol. 72, . 6 (Feb 2023).

title: COVID-19 Vaccine Relative Risks — covid_vaccine_rr
text: Link SC, et al. COVID-19 Incidence Death Rates Among Unvaccinated Fully Vaccinated Adults Without Booster Doses Periods Delta Omicron Variant Emergence. MMWR 2023;72:132-138. https://www.cdc.gov/mmwr/volumes/72/wr/mm7206a3.htm

title: COVID-19 Vaccine Relative Risks — covid_vaccine_rr
code: covid_vaccine_rr() #> # A tibble: 15 × 8 #>    age_group vaccination_status deaths_per_100k micromorts microlives #>    <chr>     <chr>                        <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 All ages  Unvaccinated                 2          20          14   #>  2 All ages  Monovalent only              0.4         4           2.8 #>  3 All ages  Bivalent booster             0.1         1           0.7 #>  4 18-49     Unvaccinated                 0.1         1           0.7 #>  5 18-49     Monovalent only              0.02        0.2         0.1 #>  6 18-49     Bivalent booster             0.005       0.05        0   #>  7 50-64     Unvaccinated                 0.8         8           5.6 #>  8 50-64     Monovalent only              0.2         2           1.4 #>  9 50-64     Bivalent booster             0.1         1           0.7 #> 10 65-79     Unvaccinated                 7.6        76          53.2 #> 11 65-79     Monovalent only              0.9         9           6.3 #> 12 65-79     Bivalent booster             0.3         3           2.1 #> 13 80+       Unvaccinated                23.4       234         164.  #> 14 80+       Monovalent only              5.5        55          38.5 #> 15 80+       Bivalent booster             2.3        23          16.1 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: relative_risk <dbl>, period <chr>, source_url <chr> covid_vaccine_rr() |> dplyr::filter(age_group == "All ages") #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>   age_group vaccination_status deaths_per_100k micromorts microlives #>   <chr>     <chr>                        <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 All ages  Unvaccinated                   2           20       14   #> 2 All ages  Monovalent only                0.4          4        2.8 #> 3 All ages  Bivalent booster               0.1          1        0.7 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: relative_risk <dbl>, period <chr>, source_url <chr>

what: Daily Hazard Rate by Age — daily_hazard_rate
title: Daily Hazard Rate by Age — daily_hazard_rate
text: Calculates daily probability death based age using simplified Gompertz-Makeham mortality model.

title: Daily Hazard Rate by Age — daily_hazard_rate
Daily Hazard Rate by Age — daily_hazard_rate
text: age Age years sex "male" "female" (default: "male")

title: Daily Hazard Rate by Age — daily_hazard_rate
text: tibble : age Input age sex Input sex daily_prob Daily probability death micromorts Daily baseline risk micromorts microlives_consumed Estimated microlives consumed per day interpretation Human-readable summary

title: Daily Hazard Rate by Age — daily_hazard_rate
text: Gompertz B (1825). "Nature Function Expressive Law Human Mortality." Philosophical Transactions Royal Society.

title: Daily Hazard Rate by Age — daily_hazard_rate
code: # Baseline risk at age 30 daily_hazard_rate(30) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>     age sex   daily_prob micromorts microlives_consumed interpretation           #>   <dbl> <chr>      <dbl>      <dbl>               <dbl> <chr>                    #> 1    30 male  0.00000203          2                0.04 At age 30 (male): 2.0 m…  # Compare male vs female at age 65 daily_hazard_rate(65, "male") #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>     age sex   daily_prob micromorts microlives_consumed interpretation           #>   <dbl> <chr>      <dbl>      <dbl>               <dbl> <chr>                    #> 1    65 male   0.0000346       34.6                0.25 At age 65 (male): 34.6 … daily_hazard_rate(65, "female") #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>     age sex    daily_prob micromorts microlives_consumed interpretation          #>   <dbl> <chr>       <dbl>      <dbl>               <dbl> <chr>                   #> 1    65 female  0.0000151       15.1                0.11 At age 65 (female): 15…

title: Demographic Life Expectancy Factors — demographic_factors
text: Population-level factors affecting baseline life expectancy, expressed microlives per day relative reference population.

title: Demographic Life Expectancy Factors — demographic_factors
text: tibble demographic factors microlife effects.

title: Demographic Life Expectancy Factors — demographic_factors
text: Based actuarial data epidemiological studies.

title: Demographic Life Expectancy Factors — demographic_factors
text: Spiegelhalter D (2012). BMJ 2012;345:e8223. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microlife

title: Demographic Life Expectancy Factors — demographic_factors
code: demographic_factors() #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   factor         comparison                 microlives_per_day source source_url #>   <chr>          <chr>                                   <dbl> <chr>  <chr>      #> 1 Sex            Female vs Male                              4 BMJ 2… https://e… #> 2 Era            2010 vs 1910                               15 BMJ 2… https://e… #> 3 Country (male) Sweden vs Russia                           21 BMJ 2… https://e… #> 4 Country (male) UK vs Russia                               15 BMJ 2… https://e… #> 5 Socioeconomic  Professional vs Unskilled…                  4 BMJ 2… https://e… #> 6 Education      Degree vs No qualificatio…                  4 BMJ 2… https://e…

title: Launch Micromort REST API — launch_api
text: Starts Plumber API server accessing micromort datasets.

title: Launch Micromort REST API — launch_api
text: host Host bind (default: "127.0.0.1") port Port listen (default: 8080) docs Enable Swagger documentation (default: TRUE)

title: Launch Micromort REST API — launch_api
text: Invisible NULL. Runs API server interrupted.

title: Launch Micromort REST API — launch_api
text: API provides endpoints : GET /v1/acute - Acute risks dataset GET /v1/chronic - Chronic risks dataset GET /v1/sources - Risk sources registry GET /v1/hazard - Daily hazard rate age GET /v1/categories - Unique categories GET /v1/meta - API metadata GET /health - Health check

title: Launch Micromort REST API — launch_api
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Start the API server launch_api()  # Then in another session or browser: # curl http://localhost:8080/v1/acute # curl http://localhost:8080/v1/chronic?direction=gain # curl http://localhost:8080/v1/hazard?age=35 } # }

title: Launch Risk Explorer Dashboard — launch_dashboard
text: Starts interactive Shiny dashboard exploring micromort microlife data.

title: Launch Risk Explorer Dashboard — launch_dashboard
text: ... Additional arguments passed shiny::runApp()

title: Launch Risk Explorer Dashboard — launch_dashboard
text: Invisible NULL. Runs dashboard closed.

title: Calculate Lifestyle Tradeoff — lifestyle_tradeoff
text: Calculate much one good habit compensates bad habit.

title: Calculate Lifestyle Tradeoff — lifestyle_tradeoff
text: bad_habit Factor name bad habit (chronic_risks) good_habit Factor name compensating behavior

title: Calculate Lifestyle Tradeoff — lifestyle_tradeoff
text: tibble showing tradeoff ratio

title: Calculate Lifestyle Tradeoff — lifestyle_tradeoff
code: # How much exercise offsets 2 cigarettes? lifestyle_tradeoff("Smoking 2 cigarettes", "20 min moderate exercise") #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   bad_habit            bad_ml_per_day good_habit    good_ml_per_day units_needed #>   <chr>                         <dbl> <chr>                   <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 Smoking 2 cigarettes             -1 20 min moder…               2          0.5 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: interpretation <chr>

title: Loss of Life Expectancy (LLE) — lle
text: Estimates average time lost lifespan due specific risk.

title: Loss of Life Expectancy (LLE) — lle
text: prob Numeric. Probability death. life_expectancy Numeric. Remaining life expectancy years (default 40).

title: Loss of Life Expectancy (LLE) — lle
text: Numeric. Loss life expectancy seconds, minutes, days (estimated).

title: Loss of Life Expectancy (LLE) — lle
code: lle(1/1000000, 40) # Loss from 1 micromort #> [1] 21.0384 #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "micromort_lle" "numeric"       #> attr(,"units") #> [1] "minutes"

title: Plot Risk Comparison — plot_risks
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Risk Sources Registry — risk_sources
text: registry data sources used compile risk datasets. source unique identifier links records acute_risks chronic_risks.

text: vsl Numeric. Value Statistical Life (default $10,000,000).

text: Numeric value one micromort.

title: Value of a Statistical Life (VSL) to Micromort Value — value_of_micromort
